growth_records.medium: 13C-bicarbonate + thiosulfate amended Hainich CZE groundwater microcosm (well H41)
growth_records.cite: Taubert, Martin et al. Bolstering fitness via CO2 fixation and organic carbon uptake: mixotrophs in modern groundwater. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01163-x. [PMC8941145]
growth_records.snippet: Generation times for cluster V microbes were slightly longer and more variable, ranging from 3.5 days for species of Acidovorax to 8 days for Aquabacterium spp. (Fig. 3).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported = 3.5 days (protein-SIP, first 3 weeks incubation). CALCULATED mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/(3.5*24 h) = 0.693147/84 = 0.008252 h-1; doubling_time_h = 84. Environmental microcosm, genus-level MAG (Acidovorax), not a defined pure culture; report retained for GrowthDB ecological growth-rate scope. No per-organism uptake/secretion rates given (only community-level consumption, see community record).

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined minimal medium (NO3- reduction medium)

up.method: ion chromatography time-course; 3H11 consumes NO3- and accumulates NO2-

up.units: mM consumed (not a rate; growth on N2O was dose-independent, Ks~0, 2-30 mM)
up.method: GC headspace; 3H11 can gain biomass by reducing N2O to N2

sec.units: mM (accumulated; not reported as a rate)
sec.method: ion chromatography; 3H11 lacks a nitrite reductase (Nir), so NO2- accumulates from NO3- reduction

yields.note: NO2- strongly inhibited growth rate and biomass yield of 3H11; complete inhibition above 5 mM NO2- in 10 mM NO3- medium (Ki 3H11 = 9.11 mM)

growth_records.cite: Carr, Alex V et al. Emergence and disruption of cooperativity in a denitrifying microbial community. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf093. [PMC12146267]
growth_records.method: reported (measured by OD600 over time; 3H11 grew significantly faster than R12 across a wide NO3- range and reached max growth rate at much lower substrate conc [Ks 3H11 << Ks R12]; numeric per-hour value not in text)
growth_records.snippet: consistent with the absence of a genome-encoded NO2- reductase (Nir), 3H11 consumed NO3- and accumulated NO2- ... its growth rate on N2O was constant and not dose-dependent (2-30 mM; Ks 3H11 ~ 0).
growth_records.notes: Acidovorax sp. GW101-3H11 monoculture, anaerobic batch, 20 mM acetate + 10 mM NaNO3, 30C, pH 7.2, N2:CO2 80:20. Genome encodes Nar, Nor, Nos but NO Nir -> reduces NO3- to NO2- and accumulates NO2- (incomplete denitrifier). Also grows on N2O as sole acceptor (dose-independent, Ks~0). Experimentally measured NO3- consumption, NO2- accumulation and acetate consumption by ion chromatography over time; numeric uptake/secretion rates computed for GEM but held in GitHub repo, not in text (rate=null). NO2- inhibition kinetics reported: Ki 3H11 = 9.11 mM, complete inhibition >5 mM NO2-. Growth by OD600; no per-hour mu value in text.
